implReport.notes: TDD. validateEnvelope reconstructs only trusted fields so client workspace_id can never flow through; workspace_id solely from workspace_api_keys. Ingest returns 200 ONLY after SQS resolves (deferred-mock ordering), 503 on SQS reject, 400 bad payload (no send), 403 unknown key (no send). FIFO MessageGroupId=profile_id, MessageDeduplicationId=event_id. planProcessing orders profile-upsert BEFORE event-insert (FK ok for progress-first stub); events ON CONFLICT(event_id) DO NOTHING; profiles ON CONFLICT(workspace_id,external_id) merge attrs only on profile_created; buildEventInsert resolves profile_id via (workspace_id,external_id) subquery so event always links same profile (never client value). Handler reports partial batchItemFailures (no ack->redrive->DLQ); unparseable body reported as failure not throw. Processor isolation proven on admin/service-role pool (rolbypassrls=true) via in-code scoping + (workspace_id,external_id) key (NOT SET ROLE to non-BYPASSRLS). Scope discipline: profile_features + segment re-eval are documented no-logic extension points (Phase 4/5). turbo.json unchanged (cache:false). Unique workspace UUID namespaces per integration file.
implReport.testSummary: All 28 turbo test tasks green on TWO consecutive COLD runs. Phase-3: ingest 22 (resolve-workspace, ingest-core, durable-boundary unit + api-key-isolation integration), processor 20 + 1 skipped (core, failure-dlq unit; idempotency, ordering-convergence, workspace-isolation integration RAN on real PG; fifo-localstack e2e skipped no LocalStack). Repo total 114 passed + 1 skipped / 21 files. Integration tier confirmed running on real PG localhost:5433.

invariants: Tenant isolation is the top property: every tenant-scoped row carries workspace_id NOT NULL; every query filters by workspace_id.
Service-role Lambda code bypasses RLS — it MUST scope by workspace_id in code; RLS is only the backstop for user-context (admin app) reads.
workspace_id is NEVER taken from a client payload — derive it from the API key (ingest) or the authorizer-injected claim (admin API).
Per-profile ordering via MessageGroupId=profile_id; code must be idempotent and order-convergent (progress-before-profile_created still converges).
Idempotency: events.event_id is the dedupe key (INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING).
Segment rule-AST -> SQL is compiled with a field/operator whitelist, parameterized SQL only, and ALWAYS prepends workspace_id = $ws. Never interpolate raw input.
Local-only: verify against LocalStack + Supabase CLI/Testcontainers. Do NOT mock Postgres in the integration tier. Mock SES (never send real mail).
Scope discipline: implement exactly what the cited spec sections require — no extra features, no gold-plating.

criteria: profile_created then immediately progress (same external_id+workspace) -> profile exists, progress applied, in order.
progress-first -> idempotent upsert still correct (stub profile created, converges).
No loss: forced Processor failures retried then processed or sent to DLQ; none vanish.
An API key for Workspace A can only create events in Workspace A.
scope: API Gateway REST request model + per-workspace API key resolution (workspace_api_keys).

services/ingest: resolve workspace_id from API key (never payload), upsert profile by (workspace_id, external_id), enqueue SQS FIFO (MessageGroupId=profile_id, MessageDeduplicationId=event_id, workspace_id in body), return 200 only after SQS accepts.
services/processor: FIFO consumer — INSERT events ON CONFLICT(event_id) DO NOTHING, upsert profile (stub if progress arrives first), workspace-scoped throughout, DLQ on repeated failure.
Logic in pure injected functions; mock SQS/AWS with aws-sdk-client-mock; integration against local Postgres.
phase.title: Ingestion + tenancy + ordering

implUnits: 2. services/ingest/src/handler.ts thin (SQSClient+pool injected, 200 only after SQS)
4. services/processor/src/handler.ts thin (batch item failure on error, no ack)
plan.notes: Reuse Phase-2 harness (hasDatabaseUrl+skipIf, adminPool seeds via service-role, applyMigrations). KEY: Processor runs as SERVICE ROLE bypassing RLS, so workspace-isolation integration test runs on admin pool and proves isolation from in-code scopedQuery + (workspace_id,external_id) key, NOT RLS (mirror api service-role-scoping test; do NOT SET ROLE to non-BYPASSRLS for processor). Don't test 'SQS delivered in order' (§16A); test idempotency/convergence + mock SQS for durable-boundary + group/dedup ids. Durable-boundary is load-bearing: 200 strictly after SQS resolves; rejected send -> non-2xx so producer retries same event_id. Ingest and processor profile upserts share the SAME (workspace_id,external_id) ON CONFLICT key so they converge. LocalStack E2E guard-skipped.

assertions: resolveWorkspaceId maps request-context api_key_id->workspace_id, never payload
INSERT events ON CONFLICT(event_id) DO NOTHING
created+later progress converge to same profile_id
real PG: same event_id twice -> 1 events row
progress-first: stub then created upserts same profile_id
service-role pool: ws-A and ws-B both have external_id cust-1; event for ws-A lands only ws-A
admin conn sees both (bypasses RLS) yet processor scopes to ws-A in code
real PG: key-A->ws-A, key-B->ws-B; key-A upserts only ws-A
key-A + external_id existing in ws-B touches only ws-A
created then progress through FIFO -> converged in real PG

instructions: Independently RUN the tests (pnpm test for the phase scope), pnpm typecheck, pnpm lint. Record real pass/fail.

Verify EACH acceptance criterion is actually covered by a passing test: ["profile_created then immediately progress (same external_id+workspace) -> profile exists, progress applied, in order.","progress-first -> idempotent upsert still correct (stub profile created, converges).","No loss: forced Processor failures retried then processed or sent to DLQ; none vanish.","Idempotency: repeated event_id applied once.","An API key for Workspace A can only create events in Workspace A."].

Tenant-isolation review (highest priority): confirm workspace_id scoping in code on every query, that RLS policies are correct, that service-role paths still scope in code, and that workspace_id is never read from client input. For phase5, verify the SQL compiler whitelists fields/operators, parameterizes, and ALWAYS injects workspace_id.
Scope check: flag any feature implemented beyond the cited spec sections.
If integration tests mock Postgres, that is a FAIL (must use real local Postgres per §16A).
Score 0-100. Set testsPass and criteriaAllMet honestly. Give concrete prioritized recommendations.
prompt.outputFormat: JSON: { score: number, testsPass: boolean, criteriaAllMet: boolean, criteriaCoverage: [{criterion, met:boolean, evidence}], isolationOk: boolean, scopeCreep: string[], recommendations: string[], criticalIssues: string[] }
prompt.role: adversarial principal reviewer + QA engineer verifying spec-fidelity and tenant isolation
prompt.task: Independently verify "Ingestion + tenancy + ordering" against its §18 criteria. Re-run the tests yourself; do not trust the self-report.
